info.description:
# Overview

The AgentKit API gives your AI agents authenticated access to third-party apps — sending emails, reading calendars, creating tickets, querying databases, and more. Your agent calls a tool; Scalekit handles the OAuth flow, token storage, and API call.

**Base URLs:**

```
https://{your-subdomain}.scalekit.dev (Development)
https://{your-subdomain}.scalekit.com (Production)
```

## Quickstart

### 1. Get an access token

Use your API credentials from the [Scalekit Dashboard](https://app.scalekit.com) → **Developers → Settings → API Credentials**.

```sh
curl -X POST https://<SCALEKIT_ENVIRONMENT_URL>/oauth/token \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  -d 'client_id={client_id}' \
  -d 'client_secret={client_secret}' \
  -d 'grant_type=client_credentials'
```

### 2. List connected accounts

```sh
curl https://<SCALEKIT_ENVIRONMENT_URL>/api/v1/connected_accounts \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer {access_token}'
```

### 3. Execute a tool on behalf of a user

```sh
curl -X POST https://<SCALEKIT_ENVIRONMENT_URL>/api/v1/execute_tool \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer {access_token}' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "connected_account_id": "{connected_account_id}",
    "tool_name": "gmail_fetch_emails",
    "tool_input": { "max_results": 5 }
  }'
```

## SDKs

```sh
npm install @scalekit-sdk/node    # Node.js
pip install scalekit-sdk-python    # Python
```

For the full product guide, see the [AgentKit documentation](https://docs.scalekit.com/agentkit/quickstart/).

---

Looking for SSO, SCIM, directory sync, or user management APIs? See the [SaaSKit API reference](https://docs.scalekit.com/saaskit/apis/). For the complete endpoint list across both products, see [All APIs](https://docs.scalekit.com/apis/).
info.title: AgentKit APIs

x-scalar-sdk-installation.description: Set up OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials authentication to access Scalekit APIs. Includes credential configuration, token exchange, and authenticated API request examples.
x-scalar-sdk-installation.source:
# 1. Obtain API Credentials
# Get your credentials from the Scalekit dashboard
export SCALEKIT_ENVIRONMENT_URL="https://your-org.scalekit.dev" # Your Scalekit environment URL
export SCALEKIT_CLIENT_ID="your_client_id"                      # Your client ID
export SCALEKIT_CLIENT_SECRET="your_client_secret"              # Your client secret

# 2. Exchange client credentials an OAuth 2.0 access token
TOKEN_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST "${SCALEKIT_ENVIRONMENT_URL}/oauth/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "client_id=${SCALEKIT_CLIENT_ID}" \
  -d "client_secret=${SCALEKIT_CLIENT_SECRET}" \
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials")

# 3. Make Authenticated API Requests
curl -X GET "${SCALEKIT_ENVIRONMENT_URL}/api/v1/organizations" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ACCESS_TOKEN}" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"

tags.name: Connected Accounts
tags.description: Manage connected accounts for third-party integrations and OAuth connections. Connected accounts represent authenticated access to external services like Google, Notion, Slack, and other applications.

tags.name: Connectors
tags.description: Manage built-in and custom connectors (providers) that agents can use to authenticate against third-party services.

tags.name: Tool Calling
tags.description: Execute tools on behalf of users via their connected accounts. Supports both named/pre-defined tools and custom API proxy calls.

tags.name: MCP Configurations
tags.description: Create and manage reusable MCP configurations.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of connected accounts for third-party integrations. Filter by organization, user, connector type, provider, or identifier. Returns OAuth tokens, API keys, and connection status for each account. Use pagination tokens to navigate through large result sets.

parameters.description: Filter by organization ID. Returns only connected accounts associated with this organization.
parameters.name: organization_id

parameters.description: Filter by user ID. Returns only connected accounts associated with this user.
parameters.name: user_id

parameters.description: Filter by connector type (e.g., 'notion', 'slack', 'google'). Alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, and colons are allowed.
parameters.name: connector

parameters.description: Filter by account identifier. The unique identifier for the connected account within the third-party service (e.g., email address, workspace ID).
parameters.name: identifier

parameters.description: Filter by OAuth provider. The authentication provider name such as 'google', 'microsoft', 'github', etc.
parameters.name: provider

parameters.description: Maximum number of connected accounts to return per page. Must be between 0 and 100. Default is typically 10.
parameters.name: page_size

parameters.description: Pagination token from a previous response. Use the next_page_token value from ListConnectedAccountsResponse to fetch the next page.
parameters.name: page_token

parameters.description: Text search query to filter connected accounts by name, identifier, or other searchable fields. Case-insensitive.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Filter by one or more connection names (exact match). Returns connected accounts belonging to any of the specified connections. Max 20 names per request. Cannot be combined with the `connector` field.
parameters.name: connection_names

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of connected accounts with their authentication details and status

400.description: Invalid request - occurs when query parameters are malformed or validation fails

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

put.description: Updates authentication credentials and configuration for an existing connected account. Modify OAuth tokens, refresh tokens, access scopes, or API configuration settings. Specify the account by ID, or by combination of organization/user, connector, and identifier. Returns the updated account with new token expiry and status information.

200.description: Connected account updated successfully with new credentials or configuration

400.description: Invalid request - missing required fields, invalid authorization details, or validation failed

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

404.description: Connected account not found - the specified account does not exist

post.description: Creates a new connected account with OAuth tokens or API credentials for third-party service integration. Supply authorization details including access tokens, refresh tokens, scopes, and optional API configuration. The account can be scoped to an organization or user. Returns the created account with its unique identifier and authentication status.

201.description: Connected account created successfully with authentication credentials stored securely

400.description: Invalid request - missing required fields, invalid authorization details, or validation failed

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

409.description: Conflict - connected account with the same identifier already exists

get.description: Retrieves complete authentication details for a connected account including OAuth tokens, refresh tokens, scopes, and API configuration. Query by account ID or by combination of organization/user, connector, and identifier. Returns sensitive credential information - use appropriate access controls.

parameters.description: Organization ID for the connector
parameters.name: organization_id

parameters.description: User ID for the connector
parameters.name: user_id

parameters.description: Connector identifier (e.g., 'notion', 'slack', 'google'). Alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, and colons are allowed.
parameters.name: connector

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the connected account within the third-party service (e.g., email address, user ID, workspace identifier).
parameters.name: identifier

parameters.description: Unique identifier for the connected account
parameters.name: id

200.description: Successfully retrieved connected account with full authentication details

400.description: Invalid request - missing required query parameters

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

404.description: Connected account not found - no account matches the specified criteria

get.description: Returns metadata for a connected account including status, connector type, provider, and configuration without exposing stored authorization credentials. Look up by account ID, or by a combination of organization (or user), connector, and external identifier.

parameters.description: Organization ID for the connector
parameters.name: organization_id

parameters.description: User ID for the connector
parameters.name: user_id

parameters.description: Connector identifier (e.g., 'notion', 'slack', 'google'). Alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, and colons are allowed.
parameters.name: connector

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the connected account within the third-party service (e.g., email address, user ID, workspace identifier).
parameters.name: identifier

parameters.description: Unique identifier for the connected account
parameters.name: id

200.description: Successfully retrieved connected account details

400.description: Invalid request - missing required query parameters

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

404.description: Connected account not found - no account matches the specified criteria

post.description: Creates a time-limited magic link for connecting or re-authorizing a third-party account. The link directs users to the OAuth authorization flow for the specified connector. Returns the generated link URL and expiration timestamp. Links typically expire after a short duration for security.

200.description: Magic link generated successfully with authorization URL and expiry time

400.description: Invalid request - missing required parameters or invalid connector

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

post.description: Confirms the user assertion and activates the connected account after the user completes third-party OAuth. Called by the B2B app server with auth_request_id and identifier. Validates that the asserted identifier matches the one stored on the auth request and promotes pending tokens to live.

200.description: Verification successful; connected account is now ACTIVE

400.description: Invalid request - missing or malformed fields

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing access token

404.description: Not found - no pending flow for the given auth_request_id or already consumed

post.description: Permanently removes a connected account and revokes all associated authentication credentials. Identify the account by ID, or by combination of organization/user, connector, and identifier. This action cannot be undone. All OAuth tokens and API keys for this account will be invalidated.

200.description: Connected account deleted successfully and all credentials revoked

400.description: Invalid request - malformed parameters or validation failed

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

404.description: Connected account not found - the specified account does not exist

get.description: Search for connected accounts in your environment using a text query that matches against identifiers, providers, or connectors. The search performs case-insensitive matching across account details. Returns paginated results with account status and authentication type information.

parameters.description: Search term to match against connected account identifiers, providers, or connectors. Must be at least 3 characters. Case insensitive.
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Maximum number of connected accounts to return per page. Value must be between 1 and 30.
parameters.name: page_size

parameters.description: Token from a previous response for pagination. Provide this to retrieve the next page of results.
parameters.name: page_token

parameters.description: Connection ID to filter connected accounts
parameters.name: connection_id

200.description: Successfully retrieved matching connected accounts with pagination support

400.description: Invalid request - query parameter is too short (minimum 3 characters) or validation failed

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

post.description: Creates an environment-scoped custom provider (connector) with authentication patterns and optional proxy configuration. The returned identifier must be used for all subsequent update and delete operations on this provider.

201.description: Returns the newly created custom provider, including its system-generated identifier and configuration

400.description: Invalid request - the provider payload failed validation (e.g. missing required fields or invalid proxy URL)

schema.description: Details of the custom connected app provider to create. Identifier is derived by the system and the identifier returned in the response must be used for update and delete operations.

put.description: Updates an existing environment-scoped custom provider (connector) by its identifier. Only the fields provided in the request are modified.

parameters.description: Identifier of the custom connected app provider to update.
parameters.name: identifier

200.description: Returns the updated custom provider with its current configuration

400.description: Invalid request - the update payload failed validation

404.description: Not Found - no custom provider exists with the given identifier

schema.description: Fields to update for the custom connected app provider. This aligns with the create custom provider payload.

delete.description: Deletes an environment-scoped custom provider (connector) by its identifier. This operation is permanent and removes the provider definition from the environment.

200.description: The custom provider was successfully deleted (no content returned)

404.description: Not Found - no custom provider exists with the given identifier

post.description: Executes a tool action using authentication credentials from a connected account. Specify the tool by name and provide required parameters as JSON. The connected account can be identified by ID, or by combination of organization/user, connector, and identifier. Returns the execution result data and a unique execution ID for tracking. Use this endpoint to perform actions like sending emails, creating calendar events, or managing resources in external services.
post.summary: Execute a tool using a connected account

200.description: Tool executed successfully with result data and execution ID

400.description: Invalid request - occurs when tool name is missing, parameters are malformed, or tool definition validation fails

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

404.description: Tool or connected account not found - occurs when the specified tool name or connected account does not exist

500.description: Tool execution failed - occurs when the external service returns an error or the tool encounters a runtime exception

get.description: Lists MCP configurations for the current environment with optional filters for id, name prefix, and provider.

parameters.description: Filter by MCP configuration id
parameters.name: filter.id

parameters.description: Case-insensitive exact match on configuration name. Allowed characters: letters (a–z, A–Z), digits (0–9), hyphens (-), and underscores (_). Maximum 100 characters.
parameters.name: filter.name

parameters.description: Filter configs that include this provider
parameters.name: filter.provider

parameters.description: Filter configs by MCP server URL. The UUID is extracted from the last path segment of the URL and used to find the matching configuration.
parameters.name: filter.mcp_server_url

parameters.description: Look ahead text search on config name
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Number of configs to return per page (max 30)
parameters.name: page_size

parameters.description: Pagination token to fetch the next or previous page
parameters.name: page_token

200.description: Paginated list of MCP configurations

400.description: Invalid request - bad filter or pagination parameters

post.description: Creates a new MCP configuration with a set of connections and tools.

400.description: Invalid request - missing required fields or invalid connection/tool mappings

401.description: Authentication required - missing or invalid access token

get.description: Returns a single MCP configuration for the current environment by ID.

parameters.description: ID of the MCP configuration to fetch
parameters.name: config_id

200.description: The requested MCP configuration

404.description: Not Found - MCP configuration does not exist

put.description: Updates the description and connection-to-tool mappings for an existing MCP configuration. The configuration name cannot be changed after creation.

parameters.description: ID of the MCP configuration to update
parameters.name: config_id

200.description: The updated MCP configuration

400.description: Invalid request - malformed payload or invalid mappings

404.description: Not Found - MCP configuration does not exist

delete.description: Deletes the MCP configuration and any associated mappings and instances in the current environment.

parameters.description: ID of the MCP configuration to delete
parameters.name: config_id

200.description: MCP configuration and associated data deleted successfully

404.description: Not Found - MCP configuration does not exist

post.description: Returns the connected account state for each connection in the MCP configuration for the given user identifier. When include_auth_link is true, creates connected accounts on the fly if they do not exist and returns a fresh authentication link per connection. When include_auth_link is false or omitted, returns the current status of existing connected accounts only — no accounts are created and authentication_link is always empty. Authentication links are only present when the connection has an associated key; if the connection has no key, authentication_link is empty regardless of include_auth_link.
post.summary: List connected accounts for an MCP configuration

parameters.description: ID of the MCP configuration
parameters.name: config_id

200.description: Connected account state returned for each connection in the configuration

400.description: Bad request - config_id or identifier is missing or invalid

404.description: Not found - no MCP configuration exists with the given config_id

post.description: Mints a short-lived JWT that represents a user identifier across the connected accounts associated with an MCP configuration. The supplied identifier becomes the token's `sub` claim; the token's `aud` claim is the MCP server URL bound to the configuration. Claims also carry the MCP configuration ID (`mcp_cfg`) and the list of resolved connected-account IDs (`ca_ids`). Use this operation to issue a single credential an MCP server can present on the user's behalf when calling provider tools. The mint fails if any connection mapped to the configuration has no active connected account for the identifier.

parameters.description: Unique ID of the MCP configuration whose connections back the token. The configuration must exist in the caller's environment.
parameters.name: mcp_config_id

200.description: Token created successfully; returns the signed JWT and its absolute expiry

400.description: Invalid request - mcp_config_id or identifier is missing or malformed, expiry is outside the 60s-24h window, the MCP configuration has no connections, or a connection has no active connected account for the supplied identifier

404.description: Not Found - no MCP configuration exists with the supplied ID in the caller's environment

post.description: Triggered when a new connected account is created

200.description: Webhook received successfully

post.description: Triggered when a connected account is updated

200.description: Webhook received successfully

post.description: Triggered when a connected account's status changes between two states (for example PENDING_AUTH to ACTIVE, ACTIVE to EXPIRED, or any state to DISCONNECTED). Not triggered on account creation.

200.description: Webhook received successfully

post.description: Triggered when a connected account is deleted

200.description: Webhook received successfully

post.description: Triggered when a magic link is generated for OAuth flow

200.description: Webhook received successfully

post.description: Triggered when OAuth tokens are successfully fetched

200.description: Webhook received successfully
Webhook received successfully
Webhook received successfully
Webhook received successfully

expiry.description: Optional token lifetime. Must be between 60s and 24h. Defaults to 1h when omitted.

identifier.description: Upstream-provider identifier (typically the user's email or provider user-id) shared by the connected accounts the token represents. A single identifier can map to one connected account per connection in the MCP configuration.
Identifier for the end user whose connected accounts to retrieve

include_auth_link.description: When true, generates a fresh authentication link for each connection and creates connected accounts if they do not exist. When false or omitted, returns existing connected account status without creating accounts or generating links.

connection_tool_mappings.description: Updated list of connection-to-tool mappings for this MCP config. Maximum 25 entries.

description.description: Updated description for the MCP configuration

connected_accountsAuthorizationDetails.title: Authentication credentials container supporting multiple auth types

trusted_idp.title: Trusted IDP federated credentials (e.g. AWS STS temporary credentials)

api_config.description: Optional JSON configuration for connector-specific API settings such as rate limits, custom endpoints, or feature flags.

authorization_details.description: Sensitive authentication credentials including access tokens, refresh tokens, and scopes. Contains either OAuth tokens or static auth details.

authorization_type.description: Type of authorization mechanism used. Specifies whether this connection uses OAuth, API keys, bearer tokens, or other auth methods.

connection_id.description: Reference to the parent connection configuration. Links this account to a specific connector setup in your environment.

connector.description: Connector identifier (e.g., 'notion', 'slack', 'salesforce'). Indicates which third-party application this account connects to.

id.description: Unique Scalekit-generated identifier for this connected account. Always prefixed with 'ca_'.

identifier.description: The unique identifier for this account in the third-party service. Typically an email address, user ID, or workspace identifier.

last_used_at.description: Timestamp when this connected account was last used to make an API call. Useful for tracking active connections.

provider.description: OAuth provider name (e.g., 'google', 'microsoft', 'github'). Identifies which authentication service manages this connection.

status.description: Current status of the connected account. Indicates if the account is active, expired, pending authorization, or pending user identity verification.

token_expires_at.description: Expiration timestamp for the access token. After this time, the token must be refreshed or re-authorized.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when this connected account was last modified. Updated whenever credentials or configuration changes.

connected_accountsConnectedAccountForLis.title: Connected account summary for list operations - excludes sensitive authorization details

identifier.description: The unique identifier for this account in the third-party service.

connected_accountsConnectorStatus.description:
- ACTIVE: Account is connected and credentials are valid
 - EXPIRED: Access token has expired and needs refresh
 - PENDING_AUTH: Account awaiting user authorization (re-auth initiated)
 - PENDING_VERIFICATION: OAuth complete; awaiting user identity verification
before activation
 - DISCONNECTED: Account has been manually disconnected
connected_accountsConnectorStatus.title: Status of a connected account indicating its current state

connected_accountsConnectorType.description:
- OAUTH: OAuth 2.0 authorization with access and refresh tokens
 - API_KEY: Static API key authentication
 - BASIC_AUTH: HTTP Basic Authentication (username/password)
 - BEARER_TOKEN: Bearer token authentication
 - CUSTOM: Custom authentication mechanism
 - BASIC: Basic authentication (alias)
 - OAUTH_M2M: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (machine-to-machine)
 - TRELLO_OAUTH1: Trello token-based OAuth1-style browser authorization
 - GOOGLE_DWD: Google Domain-Wide Delegation
 - TRUSTED_IDP: Trusted Identity Provider federation (e.g. AWS STS AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity)
 - SMART_FHIR: SMART on FHIR (SMART App Launch) — OAuth 2.0 authorization
for FHIR servers
connected_accountsConnectorType.title: Type of authentication mechanism used for the connected account

connected_account.description: Details of the connected account to create

connector.description: Connector identifier (e.g., 'notion', 'slack', 'google'). Alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, and colons are allowed.

identifier.description: The unique identifier for the connected account within the third-party service (e.g., email address, user ID, workspace identifier).

connected_account.description: The newly created connected account with its unique identifier, status, and complete authorization details including access tokens.

connector.description: Connector identifier (e.g., 'notion', 'slack', 'google'). Alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, and colons are allowed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the connected account to delete

identifier.description: The unique identifier for the connected account within the third-party service (e.g., email address, user ID, workspace identifier).

connected_account.description: The connected account with complete details including sensitive authorization credentials (access tokens, refresh tokens, scopes). Handle with appropriate access controls.

connector.description: Connector identifier (e.g., 'notion', 'slack', 'google'). Alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, and colons are allowed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the connected account

identifier.description: The unique identifier for the connected account within the third-party service (e.g., email address, user ID, workspace identifier).

state.description: Optional opaque state value. State added to the user verify redirect URL query params to validate the user verification

user_verify_url.description: B2B app's user verify redirect URL

expiry.description: Expiry timestamp for the authentication link

connected_accountsGoogleDWDAuth.description:
Google Domain-Wide Delegation authentication — used for GOOGLE_DWD connections.
Send only subject in requests; access_token, scopes, and token_expires_at are response-only.

access_token.description: OAuth access token acquired via the jwt-bearer grant. Present in responses only.

scopes.description: OAuth scopes granted to this token. Present in responses only.

subject.description: Email address of the Google Workspace user to impersonate via Domain-Wide Delegation.

token_expires_at.description: When the access token expires. Present in responses only.

connected_accounts.description: List of connected accounts matching the filter criteria. Excludes sensitive authorization details for security.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token for retrieving the next page. Empty if this is the last page. Pass this value to page_token in the next request.

prev_page_token.description: Pagination token for retrieving the previous page. Empty if this is the first page. Pass this value to page_token to go back.

total_size.description: Total count of connected accounts matching the filter criteria across all pages. Use for calculating pagination.

connected_accountsOauthToken.title: OAuth 2.0 access and refresh tokens with scopes

access_token.description: OAuth access token for API requests. Typically short-lived and must be refreshed after expiration.

domain.description: Associated domain for workspace or organization-scoped OAuth connections (e.g., Google Workspace domain).

refresh_token.description: OAuth refresh token for obtaining new access tokens. Long-lived and used to maintain persistent authorization.

scopes.description: List of granted OAuth scopes defining the permissions and access levels for this connection.

connected_accounts.description: List of connected accounts matching the search query. Excludes sensitive authorization details.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token for the next page. Empty if this is the last page.

prev_page_token.description: Pagination token for the previous page. Empty if this is the first page.

total_size.description: Total count of accounts matching the search query across all pages.

connected_accountsStaticAuth.title: Static authentication credentials for API keys, bearer tokens, or basic auth

details.description: Flexible JSON structure containing static credentials. Format varies by connector type (API key, username/password, etc.).

connected_accountsTrustedIDPAuth.description:
Trusted IDP federated authentication — used for TRUSTED_IDP connections (e.g. AWS Redshift).
Send only db_user in requests; cached temporary credentials are managed server-side and
returned only on output paths. secret_access_key and session_token are never exposed in
public API responses.

access_key_id.description: Federated access key ID issued by the trusted identity provider. Present in responses only.

db_user.description: Target database user for the federated session (required for provisioned Redshift clusters; ignored for serverless workgroups).

expiry.description: When the federated credentials expire. Present in responses only.

secret_access_key.description: Federated secret access key. Never returned in public API responses.

session_token.description: Federated session token. Never returned in public API responses.

connected_account.description: Details of the connected account to update

connector.description: Connector identifier (e.g., 'notion', 'slack', 'google'). Alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, and colons are allowed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the connected account to update

identifier.description: The unique identifier for the connected account within the third-party service (e.g., email address, user ID, workspace identifier).

connected_account.description: The updated connected account with refreshed credentials, new token expiry, and modified configuration settings.

auth_request_id.description: Auth request ID as base64url-encoded opaque token from the user verify redirect URL query params

identifier.description: Current logged in user's connected account identifier

post_user_verify_redirect_url.description: URL to redirect the user to after successful verification

expires_at.description: Absolute time at which the token expires. Equals issued_at + expiry.

token.description: Signed JWT (RS256) whose `sub` claim is the supplied identifier and whose `aud` claim is the MCP server URL bound to the configuration. Payload also carries the MCP configuration ID (`mcp_cfg`) and the resolved connected-account IDs (`ca_ids`). Signed with the calling environment's active JWT signing key.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token to fetch the next page

prev_page_token.description: Pagination token to fetch the previous page

total_size.description: Total number of configs matching the filter

connected_accounts.description: Connected account state for each connection in the configuration

connection_tool_mappings.description: List of connection-to-tool mappings for this MCP config. Maximum 25 entries.

examples: Summarizes daily emails and posts to Slack

id.description: Unique ID of the MCP config

mcp_server_url.description: URL of the MCP server for this configuration. Empty when the MCP config server URL feature is not enabled for this environment.

name.description: Unique name for the MCP configuration. Must be 1–100 characters. Allowed characters: lowercase letters (a–z), digits (0–9), hyphens (-), and underscores (_).

connected_account_id.description: Connected account backing this connection in the MCP instance context

connected_account_status.description: Authentication status for the connected account

tools.description: List of tool names linked to this connection (empty = all tools)

authentication_link.description: Fresh authentication link for the connected account. Empty when include_auth_link is false or when the connection has no associated key.

connected_account_id.description: ID of the connected account for this user and connection

connected_account_status.description: Authentication status of the connected account

providersProvider.title: Provider represents a connected app provider

is_custom.description: Indicates whether the provider is environment-scoped (custom provider)

is_custom_mcp.description: Indicates whether this is an environment-scoped MCP-based custom provider

metadata.description: Custom key-value metadata stored for this provider. Returned for all providers; defaults to {} when no metadata has been set.

agent_run_id.description: Optional. Customer-supplied identifier grouping multiple tool calls into a single agent run. Useful for correlating logs across an agentic workflow.

connected_account_id.description: Optional. The unique ID of the connected account. Use this to directly identify the connected account instead of using identifier + connector combination.

connector.description: Optional. The name of the connector/provider (e.g., 'Google Workspace', 'Slack', 'Notion'). Alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, underscores, and colons are allowed. Use this in combination with identifier to identify the connected account.

identifier.description: Optional. The unique identifier for the connected account within the third-party service (e.g., email address, user ID, workspace identifier). Use this in combination with connector to identify the connected account.

organization_id.description: Optional. The organization ID to scope the connected account lookup. Use this to narrow down the search when the same identifier exists across multiple organizations.

params.description: JSON object containing the parameters required for tool execution. The structure depends on the specific tool being executed.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool to execute

user_id.description: Optional. The user ID to scope the connected account lookup. Use this to narrow down the search when the same identifier exists across multiple users.

data.description: Free-flowing JSON parameters for the tool execution

execution_id.description: Unique identifier for the tool execution

v1connected_accountsCreateConnectedAccou.title: Payload for creating a new connected account - authorization details are optional

api_config.description: Optional JSON configuration for connector-specific API settings such as rate limits, custom API endpoints, timeouts, or feature flags.

authorization_details.description: Optional authentication credentials for the connected account. Include OAuth tokens (access_token, refresh_token, scopes) or static auth details (API keys, bearer tokens). Can be provided later via update.

v1connected_accountsUpdateConnectedAccou.title: Payload for updating an existing connected account - all fields optional

api_config.description: Updated JSON configuration for API-specific settings. Merges with existing configuration - only provided fields are modified.

authorization_details.description: Updated authentication credentials. Provide new OAuth tokens (e.g., after refresh) or updated static auth details. Only included fields will be modified.

auth_patterns.description: Authentication patterns for the connected app provider

description.description: Description of the connected app provider
examples: Connect to Google Workspace for email and calendar integration

display_name.description: Display name for the connected app provider

icon_src.description: URL for the provider icon. Should be an SVG image sized 800x800 pixels for best rendering experience.

metadata.description: Custom key-value metadata for this provider. Keys must be 3-25 characters, values must be 1-256 characters, with a maximum of 20 key-value pairs.

proxy_enabled.description: This flag indicates whether proxying is turned on for the connected app provider. When enabled, requests are routed through the provider proxy instead of being sent directly.

proxy_url.description: Proxy URL for the connected app provider. Must start with https://

examples: Connect to Google Workspace for email and calendar integration

id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook event (must be prefixed with "evt_")

environment_id.description: The environment ID where the event occurred

object.description: The type of object that triggered the webhook

data.description: The event payload (structure varies by event type)

display_name.description: Human-readable display name for the event

scopes: No scope required for client credentials flow
